description: An iPhone-sized user (375x812) who expects the search bar above the fold, autocomplete suggestions that fit the viewport, and one-liner text that meets WCAG AA contrast even under mobile typography scaling.

asserts.expression: var s=document.getElementById('event-search'); if(!s) return false; var r=s.getBoundingClientRect(); window.__searchTop=r.top; return r.top >= 0 && r.top < 812;

var i=document.getElementById('event-search'); if(!i) return false; i.focus(); i.value='Para'; i.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})); return new Promise(function(r){setTimeout(function(){r(true);},450);});
var sug=document.querySelector('.search-suggestions'); if(!sug) return true; var w=sug.getBoundingClientRect().width; window.__sugWidth=w; return w > 0 && w <= 375;

var el=document.querySelector('.event-oneliner'); if(!el) return false; function parseRgb(s){var m=s.match(/rgba?\(\s*(\d+)\s*,\s*(\d+)\s*,\s*(\d+)(?:\s*,\s*([0-9.]+))?\s*\)/);if(!m) return null;return [parseInt(m[1]),parseInt(m[2]),parseInt(m[3]),m[4]===undefined?1:parseFloat(m[4])];} function L(r,g,b){var a=[r,g,b].map(function(v){v=v/255;return v<=0.03928?v/12.92:Math.pow((v+0.055)/1.055,2.4);});return 0.2126*a[0]+0.7152*a[1]+0.0722*a[2];} var fg=parseRgb(getComputedStyle(el).color); if(!fg) return false; var bg=null; var node=el; while(node && node!==document.documentElement){var c=parseRgb(getComputedStyle(node).backgroundColor); if(c && c[3]>0){bg=c; break;} node=node.parentElement;} if(!bg) bg=[255,255,255,1]; var L1=L(fg[0],fg[1],fg[2]); var L2=L(bg[0],bg[1],bg[2]); var hi=Math.max(L1,L2), lo=Math.min(L1,L2); var ratio=(hi+0.05)/(lo+0.05); window.__mobileContrast=ratio; return ratio >= 4.5;

llm.system_prompt: You are the mobile-user. Your goals: use the site comfortably on iPhone 375px wide without layout breakage. Critique this Austin cultural events calendar site through your lens. Identify anything blocking your primary user story in <=150 words. Before claiming a selector is missing, cross-check the Ground truth JSON block — it reports per-selector DOM presence/count observed on the same page as the screenshot. If Ground truth says a selector exists, treat that as authoritative and do not contradict it without explicit screenshot evidence. Start with PASS or FAIL.
llm.goals: use the site comfortably on iPhone 375px wide without layout breakage; trust the ground-truth JSON over any impression that a selector is missing
